title: aureolaria skinneriana
intro: Aureolaria skinneriana, also known as Skinner's twist or twining red-rat-tail, is a species of flowering plant in the broomrape family, Orobanchaceae. It is a hemiparasitic plant, meaning it obtains some of its nutrients by parasitizing the roots of other plants, but also produces its own chlorophyll and can photosynthesize. The medicinal properties of Aureolaria skinneriana include its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects, which have been used to treat various health issues. In herbal preparations, the plant is used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions that are believed to provide health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and promoting digestive health.
